main_sections.title: Believe in Yourself
main_sections.poem_text:
Step up to the challenge
There is no crowd to see,
It's just you and the future
And where you want to be.

Will it pull you forward
Or push you back in fear?
Difficult are choices
When the future is getting near.

There is such ease in comfort
To maintain the status quo,
But this isn't what we are made for
This isn't how we grow.

The first step is the hardest
There is no turning back,
You just need to believe in yourself
For your future to be on track.

 Robert Langley
main_sections.poem_appreciation_summary: The poem 'Step up to the challenge' by Robert Langley is a four-stanza motivational lyric addressed directly to the reader through the second-person pronoun 'you', creating an intimate and urgent tone. The central theme is the necessity of self-belief when confronting life-changing decisions. The first stanza frames the challenge as a solitary, individual encounter with one's own future, stripping away the comfort of external validation. The second stanza introduces antithesis — the dual pull of aspiration and fear — capturing the difficulty of choice as the future approaches. The third stanza critiques the comfort zone through the symbol of the 'status quo', arguing that ease and comfort, while tempting, lead to stagnation rather than growth. The final stanza resolves the tension with an imperative: the hardest step is the first, and the only tool required is self-belief. The dominant devices are antithesis ('pull you forward / push you back'), metaphor ('the first step is the hardest' — likening the act of beginning to physical effort), and direct address. The rhyme scheme is ABCB in each stanza, lending the poem a steady, forward-moving rhythm that mirrors its message of perseverance.

reading_for_comprehension.section_name: Reflect and Respond + Reading for Appreciation + Check Your Understanding + Critical Reflection

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Imagine that you are the person in the image. 1. What emotions do you feel standing at the base of a difficult task? 2. What might make you take the first step?

question_bank.stem: What emotions do you feel standing at the base of a difficult task?
What might make you take the first step?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Think about a time when you had to face a challenge. 1. What was it, and how did you feel at the start of the journey? 2. How did you feel once you made the decision to move forward?

question_bank.stem: What was the challenge, and how did you feel at the start of the journey?
How did you feel once you made the decision to move forward?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: What does the phrase 'believe in yourself' mean to you? Write some words or phrases you associate with believing in yourself.

Select the correct meaning of 'status quo' based on the given sentence. 'Even though some kids wanted to try new activities, most of them were happy with the status quo and didn't want any changes.' 1. A plan to make things more exciting. 2. A situation to keep things the same. 3. A decision where everything is completely different. 4. A choice to change things quickly without thinking.

question_bank.stem: Even though some kids wanted to try new activities, most of them were happy with the status quo and didn't want any changes.
options: A plan to make things more exciting
A situation to keep things the same
A decision where everything is completely different
A choice to change things quickly without thinking

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Based on your understanding of the poem, select the correct central idea for each stanza from the options given.

question_bank.stem: Stanza 1: (i) Facing challenges requires personal responsibility and a clear focus on one's future. (ii) Facing challenges is a journey best taken with support and guidance from others.
options: Facing challenges requires personal responsibility and a clear focus on one's future
Facing challenges is a journey best taken with support and guidance from others

question_bank.stem: Stanza 2: (i) Fear and uncertainty make it difficult to make choices as the future approaches. (ii) Facing the future with confidence makes choices easier and more straightforward.
options: Fear and uncertainty make it difficult to make choices as the future approaches
Facing the future with confidence makes choices easier and more straightforward

question_bank.stem: Stanza 3: (i) Personal growth involves finding balance between comfort and change. (ii) Personal growth requires leaving behind comfort and embracing change.
options: Personal growth involves finding balance between comfort and change
Personal growth requires leaving behind comfort and embracing change

question_bank.stem: Stanza 4: (i) The first step towards change may feel easy if you place your trust in others to guide you. (ii) The first step towards change is difficult, but having self-belief and confidence helps you stay on track.
options: The first step towards change may feel easy if you place your trust in others
The first step towards change is difficult, but having self-belief and confidence helps you stay on track

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Rhyme Scheme: Fill in the blank to complete the following sentence. The poem follows a simple, yet effective rhyme scheme _________ that flows steadily through each stanza.

Tone: State whether the following statements are true or false. 1. The overall tone of the poem is motivational and encouraging. 2. The tone shifts from thoughtful in the beginning to one of determination by the end of the poem.

question_bank.stem: The overall tone of the poem is motivational and encouraging.
The tone shifts from thoughtful in the beginning to one of determination by the end of the poem.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Speaker: Fill in the blanks with the correct options from those given in brackets. The speaker in this poem is not distant; rather, he/she comes across as a _________ (stranger/guide) who understands the struggle and is encouraging the reader to take ____________ (interest in/control of) his/her own future. The use of direct address 'You' creates a close connection, as though the speaker is __________. (talking directly to the reader/addressing the reader from a distance)

question_bank.stem: The speaker comes across as a _________ (stranger/guide).
The speaker is encouraging the reader to take ____________ (interest in/control of) his/her own future.
The use of direct address 'You' creates a close connection, as though the speaker is __________. (talking directly to the reader/addressing the reader from a distance)

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Imagery: Match the phrases from the poem in Column 1 with the imagery they represent in Column 2. An extra representation is given.

question_bank.stem: 1. 'There is no crowd to see...' — matches which imagery?
2. 'push you back in fear?' — matches which imagery?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Symbolism: Select the words/phrases from the box (unknown, self-improvement, courage, leap of faith, stagnation) to complete the given sentences. 1. Comfort and the status quo represent ___________ and fear of change. 2. The future symbolises the ____________, the potential for change that requires ___________to step into. 3. The first step symbolises the initial ___________ required to begin the journey of _____________ or personal development.

question_bank.stem: Comfort and the status quo represent ___________ and fear of change, symbolising the comfort zone that holds one back.
The future symbolises the ____________, the potential for change and success that lies ahead but requires ___________to step into.
The first step symbolises the initial ___________ required to begin the journey of _____________ or personal development.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Metaphor: The poet uses a metaphor in the line, 'The first step is the hardest'. Explain why this is metaphorical.
The poem uses antithesis in a couple of lines. [Explanation of antithesis with examples provided.] Identify the lines from the poem that show antithesis and explain why it is so.
Read the extract given below and answer the questions that follow. Extract: 'Step up to the challenge / There is no crowd to see, / It's just you and the future / And where you want to be.'

question_bank.stem: What does the line, 'There is no crowd to see' suggest about facing challenges?
Complete the following suitably. The line 'It's just you and the future' suggests that _________.
Fill in the blank with the appropriate word/phrase from the extract. Latha will ________ her efforts to improve her vocal performance by practicing harder each day.
Select the most suitable title for the extract. A. The Struggles of Change B. Facing the Future Alone C. A Journey of Growth D. The Power of Fear
Complete the analogy by using a word from the extract. achieve: goal : : face : ___________

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Answer the following questions.

question_bank.stem: What is the significance of the metaphor, 'The first step is the hardest' in the context of personal growth?
What message does the antithesis in the poem convey about the nature of personal development?

Do you think the poet's message is realistic in the context of real-world struggles? (Clue: Evaluate whether simply 'believing in yourself' is enough to overcome obstacles or other factors are also necessary.)
Consider a situation where you or someone you know had to take a difficult first step towards a goal. How does the poem's message about the importance of self-belief apply to this situation?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: The phrase 'status quo' is a Latin expression. There are other Latin terms commonly used in English. Read the given table of Latin expressions (et cetera, exempli gratia, ad hoc, in media res, per se, quid pro quo) and their meanings. Now, fill in the blanks in the given sentences with the Latin expressions from the table.

question_bank.stem: I enjoy reading fantasy books, _________ Harry Potter and Magical Paint Brush.
After helping Tanya with the homework, Ritu asked for a _________ to borrow her notes next time.
I love all kinds of outdoor activities, such as trekking, hiking, biking, _________.
The park is not very special _________; it becomes more fun when you visit with friends.
We created an _________ team to organise the school festival.
The movie started _________, with the hero already fighting the villain in a huge battle.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: The line 'Will it pull you forward/or push you back in fear?' is a rhetorical question. [Definition provided.] Read the following rhetorical questions and state what they intend to achieve: (i) 'Isn't it obvious that we must act now? Don't we all have a responsibility to make a change?' (ii) 'Will we let fear control us, or will we rise above it?'

question_bank.stem: Isn't it obvious that we must act now? Don't we all have a responsibility to make a change? — State what this rhetorical question intends to achieve.
Will we let fear control us, or will we rise above it? — State what this rhetorical question intends to achieve.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Match the situations in Column 1 to the rhetorical questions in Column 2.

question_bank.stem: (i) Deciding whether to stand up for what is right — A/B/C/D/E/F?
(ii) Owning up to a mistake made in a group project — A/B/C/D/E/F?
(iii) Deciding whether to try something challenging, like public speaking — A/B/C/D/E/F?
(iv) Choosing between two career paths — A/B/C/D/E/F?
(v) Deciding whether to apologise for a mistake — A/B/C/D/E/F?
(vi) Trying something new and stepping out of your comfort zone — A/B/C/D/E/F?

listening.transcript_text: A conversation between two friends, Karan (boy) and Ananya (girl), before Karan's school play. Karan is nervous, doubting himself and worrying he will forget his lines or freeze on stage. Ananya reassures him that he has been practising for weeks and has done many rehearsals. She tells him that self-belief is the first step: if he does not believe he can do it, he will never succeed. When Karan raises the fear of freezing under pressure, Ananya says pressure is part of the process and is what helps a person grow — the more he pushes himself, the more confident he will become. Karan admits he keeps doubting himself even though he has put in the work. Ananya counters that self-doubt is a normal part of big moments; confidence comes from taking action even when one does not feel fully ready. Karan resolves to give his best shot despite his nerves. Ananya closes by telling him he has all the tools he needs and to believe in himself and own the stage.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will listen to a conversation between two friends. As you listen, answer the following questions in one to three exact words that you hear.

question_bank.stem: How did the boy feel before the play?
According to the girl, where does confidence come from?
What was the girl finally sure about regarding the boy?

tasks_verbatim.task_text: You will once again listen to the conversation. As you listen, select the four true statements from 1–7 given below.

question_bank.stem: 1. The boy feels confident but is worried about freezing on stage.
2. The girl encourages the boy to push through his doubts by trusting in his preparation.
3. The boy thinks that the audience will be supportive regardless of his performance.
4. The girl believes that self-doubt is a normal part of preparing for a big performance.
5. The boy feels that his preparation is not enough and doubts his abilities, even though he has practiced.
6. The girl suggests that the boy should avoid feeling nervous and perform perfectly.
7. The girl believes that pushing through nervousness will help the boy grow and build confidence.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Work in pairs. Read the three Sayings/Proverbs given below. For each one, think of a real-life situation where it could apply. Do the suggested role play and use the Saying/Proverb. Proverbs: 1. Don't judge a book by its cover. 2. Actions speak louder than words. 3. When the going gets tough, the tough get going.

question_bank.stem: Proverb 1: Don't judge a book by its cover. Role-play: one student judges others by appearance; the other shows true personality after deeper conversation.
Proverb 2: Actions speak louder than words. Role-play: one student talks about improving grades; the other shows actual effort. Compare talking vs. doing.
Proverb 3: When the going gets tough, the tough get going. Role-play: one student gives up on a tough project; the other encourages perseverance.

tasks_verbatim.task_text: Your class is conducting the morning assembly. You have been asked to deliver a speech on the topic, 'Turning Challenges into Opportunities'. Draft this speech following the given guidelines: opening paragraph (greeting, quotation/question/fact, purpose/overview), paragraphs 2–3 body (two main points, transition words, anecdotes, statistics), concluding paragraph (summary, lasting statement, thanks). Use formal language and persuasive tone.

Explore the stories of Indian leaders who faced significant challenges and turned them into opportunities for success. Examples given: Dr. B. R. Ambedkar, Lal Bahadur Shastri, Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam. Now, find out about such personalities from your village, town, city, or state and present their success stories in class.

question_bank.stem: Research the story of Dr. B.R. Ambedkar and how he overcame discrimination to become the chairperson of the Drafting Committee of the Constitution of India.
Research Lal Bahadur Shastri's journey from hardship and imprisonment during the freedom movement to becoming the Prime Minister of India.

Research Dr. APJ Abdul Kalam's story of delivering newspapers to support his education and rising to become the 11th President of India. Find a local personality with a similar story and present it in class.
